description: AA-03.3 interference, timescale separation, and tail-SLO fallback gate validation for multi-controller runtime.

interference_model.description: Controllers targeting overlapping seams may create feedback loops. Interference is detected by monitoring decision oscillation across paired controllers.

overlap_pairs.feedback_risk: SCHED parks worker -> capacity drop -> ADMISSION tightens -> throughput drop -> SCHED wakes worker
RETRY steepens backoff -> timer count drops -> SCHED parks idle worker -> retry completes -> burst of ready tasks
ADMISSION tightens -> retries queue up -> RETRY flattens backoff -> burst of tasks -> ADMISSION tightens further

timescale_separation.description: Controllers must operate at distinct timescales to prevent oscillation. Faster controllers must have strictly shorter epoch intervals than slower ones.

tiers.rationale: Scheduler decisions must respond within a single epoch for latency control.
Admission changes need multiple epochs to observe steady-state impact.
Retry tuning must observe full retry cycles before adjusting.

timescale_separation.separation_invariant: For tiers i < j, tier_j.epoch_multiplier > tier_i.epoch_multiplier

tail_slo_fallback.description: When tail latency exceeds the SLO threshold, all non-conservative controllers must fall back within the fallback_deadline_epochs.

slo_fields.threshold_description: p99 task scheduling latency above target

recovery_protocol: 1. All non-Shadow controllers rolled back to Shadow
2. Fallback flag set on each rolled-back controller
3. Conservative comparator values take effect immediately
4. Recovery requires re-calibration and fresh promotion pipeline
5. Fallback flag cleared only after calibration score exceeds threshold

sequential_validity.description: Multi-controller decisions within one epoch must be applied sequentially, not concurrently. Each controller sees the state after the previous controller's decision.
sequential_validity.ordering_rule: Controllers are applied in registration order (by ControllerId). A controller's decision is recorded before the next controller observes.

drift_alarm.description: If a controller's decision would be different under the pre-decision snapshot vs post-decision snapshot of the previous controller, a drift alarm is raised.

smoke_scenarios.description: Validate interference detection across controller pairs
smoke_scenarios.command: rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-codex-aa033 cargo test --test controller_interference_validation_contract interference -- --nocapture
rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-codex-aa033 cargo test --test controller_interference_validation_contract timescale -- --nocapture

smoke_scenarios.description: Validate tail-SLO fallback gates and recovery
smoke_scenarios.command: rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-codex-aa033 cargo test --test controller_interference_validation_contract fallback -- --nocapture

smoke_scenarios.description: Validate sequential decision ordering and drift alarms
smoke_scenarios.command: rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-codex-aa033 cargo test --test controller_interference_validation_contract sequential -- --nocapture
